description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Saitama Prefecture will start the "Hazard Map by Voice" service on May 1, 2026 (Friday, Reiwa 8). This is a service that provides hazard map information—such as flooding and mudslide risks—by voice for people with visual impairments and others.

【Main features】
• Voice reading of elevation and disaster risks (flood depth, time until water level drops, etc.) for any location such as your current position or home
• Voice and vibration guidance to the nearest evacuation shelter
• Voice reading of weather alerts and warnings from the Japan Meteorological Agency

【How to use】
Install and use the free app "Uni-Voice Blind". It is a voice code app that reads "Uni-Voice" codes and can be installed from each app store.

By checking disaster risks and evacuation shelters in advance, you can carry out evacuation actions smoothly.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/somubu/bosaikikikanri/5/13507.html
summary: ℹ️ New "Hazard Map by Voice" service launches May 1, 2026
